purpose: Provides observable indicators for monitoring proximity to the nonlinear reserve scarcity threshold
components: SOFR-IORB spread: highest-frequency early warning indicator for reserve scarcity
Fed funds effective rate relative to IORB: stress indicator for reserve distribution
FHLB advance usage: leading indicator for bank liquidity stress
RRP facility level: measures remaining buffer (now near floor at <$200B)
TGA balance volatility: creates temporary reserve drains independent of QT
Intraday payment flow data: captures distributional stress invisible in aggregate
current_assessment: RRP is near operational floor (<$200B). SOFR-IORB spread should be monitored for sustained deviation. Fed staff estimates reserve floor at $2.5-3.5T with $1T uncertainty band. System is in transition from ample to possibly-approaching-adequate reserves.
limitations: Fed's own models missed the 2019 threshold by factor of 2x
Distributional effects invisible in aggregate metrics
Sell-side point estimates ($2.8-3.2T) are false precision given model uncertainty
Indicators may only signal stress after the nonlinear kink is breached
SRF usage dynamics under stress are unknown
